questions.question: A girl uses a ruler to measure the length of a metal rod. The end of the ruler is damaged, so she places one end of the rod at the 1 cm mark, as shown. 12345678 cm metal rod damaged end How long is the metal rod? A 43 mm B 46 mm C 53 mm D 56 mm

options: A 43 mm B 46 mm C 53 mm D 56 mm

questions.question: Four balls with different masses are dropped from the heights shown. Air resistance can be ignored. Which ball has the greatest average speed? 4.0 kg A 1.0 m 3.0 kg B 2.0 m 2.0 kg C 3.0 m 1.0 kg D 4.0 m ground

questions.question: A child has four blocks of wood in the shapes of the letters A, M, T and X. The blocks have equal heights and thicknesses. At their widest parts, their widths are equal. The child stands the four blocks the right way up on a flat table. Which block is most likely to fall over when the block is slightly knocked? A letter A, B letter M, C letter T, D letter X

questions.question: When an object falls freely due to gravity, energy that was stored as gravitational potential energy is transferred to the kinetic energy store. How is the energy transferred in this process? A by electrical work, B by electromagnetic waves, C by heating, D by mechanical work 5

questions.question: A woman pushes a mower across a lawn. Which quantities does the woman use to calculate the work she does? A distance, force and time, B distance and force only, C distance and time only, D force and time only
questions.text: A woman pushes a mower across a lawn. Which quantities does the woman use to calculate the work she does? A distance, force and time, B distance and force only, C distance and time only, D force and time only

questions.question: A steel knife needs a cutting edge with a small surface area. What is the reason for this? A A small surface area increases the pressure., B A small surface area increases the density of the steel., C A small surface area prevents the cutting edge from rusting., D A small surface area prevents the knife from becoming magnetised.
questions.text: A steel knife needs a cutting edge with a small surface area. What is the reason for this? A A small surface area increases the pressure., B A small surface area increases the density of the steel., C A small surface area prevents the cutting edge from rusting., D A small surface area prevents the knife from becoming magnetised.
options: A A small surface area increases the pressure.
B A small surface area increases the density of the steel.
C A small surface area prevents the cutting edge from rusting.
D A small surface area prevents the knife from becoming magnetised.

questions.question: When a hot gas cools, its internal energy decreases. What causes this? A a decrease in the average kinetic energy of the gas particles, B a decrease in the gravitational potential energy of the gas particles, C an increase in the average speed of the gas particles, D an increase in the average distance of separation of the gas particles
questions.text: When a hot gas cools, its internal energy decreases. What causes this? A a decrease in the average kinetic energy of the gas particles, B a decrease in the gravitational potential energy of the gas particles, C an increase in the average speed of the gas particles, D an increase in the average distance of separation of the gas particles
options: A a decrease in the average kinetic energy of the gas particles
B a decrease in the gravitational potential energy of the gas particles
C an increase in the average speed of the gas particles
D an increase in the average distance of separation of the gas particles

questions.question: The diagram shows part of a railway track. Each section of the railway track is separated by a gap. gap sections of railway track Why are these gaps necessary? A The tracks contract on cold days., B The tracks contract on warm days., C The tracks expand on cold days., D The tracks expand on warm days.
questions.text: The diagram shows part of a railway track. Each section of the railway track is separated by a gap. gap sections of railway track Why are these gaps necessary? A The tracks contract on cold days., B The tracks contract on warm days., C The tracks expand on cold days., D The tracks expand on warm days.
options: A The tracks contract on cold days.
B The tracks contract on warm days.
C The tracks expand on cold days.
D The tracks expand on warm days.

questions.question: A test-tube contains a volume of 1.0 cm 3 of liquid water at a temperature of 100 °C. The liquid water boils to form a volume of 1600 cm 3 of steam. What is the reason for the large increase in volume? A Steam particles are bigger than water particles., B The average distance between the particles is much greater in the steam., C The particles do not move until the water turns into a gas., D There are more steam particles than there were water particles.

options: A Steam particles are bigger than water particles.
B The average distance between the particles is much greater in the steam.
C The particles do not move until the water turns into a gas.
D There are more steam particles than there were water particles.

questions.question: The diagram shows a hot-water tank. The tank has a heater, X, halfway up the tank. hot water out cold water in heater X Heater X is switched on. questions.question: The handle of a metal cooking pan is made from wood. What is a reason for making the handle from wood? questions.text: The handle of a metal cooking pan is made from wood. What is a reason for making the handle from wood? options: A to increase thermal energy transfer by conduction
B to increase thermal energy transfer by convection
C to reduce thermal energy transfer by conduction
D to reduce thermal energy transfer by convection 8

questions.question: P-waves and S-waves are produced in the ground during an earthquake. Which statement about these waves is correct? A Only P-waves are transverse waves., B Only P-waves are longitudinal waves., C Both P-waves and S-waves are longitudinal waves., D Both P-waves and S-waves are transverse waves. 9
questions.text: P-waves and S-waves are produced in the ground during an earthquake. Which statement about these waves is correct? A Only P-waves are transverse waves., B Only P-waves are longitudinal waves., C Both P-waves and S-waves are longitudinal waves., D Both P-waves and S-waves are transverse waves. 9
options: A Only P-waves are transverse waves.
B Only P-waves are longitudinal waves.
C Both P-waves and S-waves are longitudinal waves.
D Both P-waves and S-waves are transverse waves. 9

questions.question: A drummer hits a drum harder to make a louder sound without changing the pitch of the sound. Which statement describes the change in properties of the sound as the drummer hits the drum harder? A Only the amplitude of the sound increases., B Only the frequency of the sound decreases., C Both the amplitude and the frequency of the sound increase., D Both the amplitude and the frequency of the sound decrease.
